pkg/framework/tracex.purpose: Provides a generic, type-safe OTel span wrapper (Span[T] and SpanNoValue) that automatically records errors, sets span status, and recovers panics — eliminating span lifecycle boilerplate from every domain service.

patterns.name: Start[T] + Wrap for value-returning operations
patterns.description: Use tracex.Start[T](ctx, tracer, name) then span.Wrap(fn) for functions returning (T, error); status is set Ok/Error automatically and panics are recorded and re-panicked after span.End().
patterns.example: span := tracex.Start[*Invoice](ctx, s.tracer, "billing.GetInvoice"); return span.Wrap(func(ctx context.Context) (*Invoice, error) { return s.adapter.Get(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: StartWithNoValue + Wrap for error-only operations
patterns.description: Use tracex.StartWithNoValue(ctx, tracer, name) then span.Wrap(fn) when the function returns only error; delegates to Span[any].Wrap to share status logic.
patterns.example: span := tracex.StartWithNoValue(ctx, s.tracer, "billing.DeleteInvoice"); return span.Wrap(func(ctx context.Context) error { return s.adapter.Delete(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: Pass the span's ctx into the callback, never the outer ctx
patterns.description: Span.Wrap passes the child span ctx into fn — always use the ctx argument inside Wrap callbacks, never capture the outer ctx, or OTel parent-child links break.
patterns.example: span.Wrap(func(ctx context.Context) (*Invoice, error) { return s.adapter.Get(ctx, id) }) // ctx is the span child ctx

patterns.name: WithOkStatusDescription for custom span descriptions
patterns.description: Pass tracex.WithOkStatusDescription(desc) as a variadic opt to Wrap for a specific success description; default is 'success'.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: Span[T], SpanNoValue, Start, StartWithNoValue, Wrap, Options. Single file; all logic here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Wrap always calls span.End() via both the panic-defer and normal paths — never call span.End() outside Wrap or the span is double-ended and OTel discards data.

anti_patterns: Calling tracer.Start directly and managing span.End/RecordError manually — use tracex.Start + Wrap.
Introducing context.Background() inside Wrap callbacks — use the ctx argument carrying the child span ctx.
Adding new Span variants that don't delegate to Span[any].Wrap — SpanNoValue shows the correct delegation for error-only wrappers.
Calling span.End() manually after span.Wrap — Wrap already ends; double-ending silently truncates the span.

decisions.decision: Generic Span[T] instead of an untyped wrapper.
decisions.rationale: Preserves compile-time type safety for the return value while sharing identical error-recording and panic-recovery logic across call sites.
The span must be closed before the stack unwinds, but the caller's defer/recover chain must still see the original panic.

code_examples.scenario: Wrap a DB read in a named span with automatic error recording

pkg/framework/transaction.purpose: Database-agnostic transaction abstraction: propagates a Driver (commit/rollback/savepoint) through context and provides Run/RunWithNoValue helpers that start-or-reuse a transaction, set savepoints for nested calls, and rollback on error or panic.

patterns.name: Run[R] for transactional value-returning operations
patterns.description: transaction.Run(ctx, creator, cb) executes cb in a transaction; if a Driver is already in ctx it is reused with a savepoint, otherwise creator.Tx starts a new one.
patterns.example: result, err := transaction.Run(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context) (*Entity, error) { return a.insertRow(ctx) })

patterns.name: RunWithNoValue for error-only transactional operations
patterns.description: Use transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, creator, cb) when the callback returns only error; delegates to Run[interface{}].
patterns.example: err := transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context) error { return a.deleteRow(ctx, id) })

patterns.name: Driver propagation via context key
patterns.description: SetDriverOnContext stores a Driver under a package-private key; GetDriverFromContext retrieves it. Run handles this automatically — adapters must not call SetDriverOnContext directly.
patterns.example: tx, err := transaction.GetDriverFromContext(ctx) // read-only; Run writes it

patterns.name: Creator interface for adapter integration
patterns.description: Any adapter that can start a transaction implements transaction.Creator via Tx(ctx) returning (ctx, Driver, error); Wire injects the concrete Creator into services.
patterns.example: type Creator interface { Tx(ctx context.Context) (context.Context, Driver, error) }

patterns.name: Savepoints for nested Run calls
patterns.description: manage() calls tx.SavePoint() before every cb execution, enabling partial rollback within an outer transaction — required for multi-step billing mutations.
patterns.example: // Automatic: every transaction.Run creates a savepoint even when reusing an existing tx

key_file_guides.role: Typed context key, GetDriverFromContext, SetDriverOnContext, DriverNotFoundError, DriverConflictError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SetDriverOnContext returns DriverConflictError (not nil) when a driver already exists — Run ignores this type to reuse the tx; do not treat it as fatal outside Run.

key_file_guides.role: Driver and Creator interfaces; Run, RunWithNoValue, getTx, manage. manage owns panic recovery (rollback + re-panic), savepoint creation, and commit/rollback on error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: manage() uses defer+recover to rollback and re-panic — never suppress this panic in adapter code. getTx silently reuses an existing Driver from ctx if present.

anti_patterns: Calling creator.Tx() directly in adapter code instead of transaction.Run — bypasses savepoint and rollback logic.
Storing a *entdb.Tx or raw DB client on adapter structs and using them directly — falls off the ctx-bound transaction.
Calling SetDriverOnContext manually outside transaction.Run — Run handles it; double-setting returns DriverConflictError and breaks nesting.
Using context.Background() inside a Run callback — loses the Driver in ctx, breaking nested transaction reuse.
Treating DriverConflictError from SetDriverOnContext as a hard error — it signals tx reuse and must not be fatal.

decisions.decision: Driver interface with SavePoint/Commit/Rollback instead of exposing *sql.Tx.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the package DB-agnostic; Ent, pgx, and future drivers implement Driver without leaking ORM types into the framework.

decisions.decision: Savepoints used for every nested Run call.
decisions.rationale: Allows partial rollback of nested operations while keeping the outer transaction alive — required for multi-step charge advancement and invoice mutations.

decisions.decision: Panic recovery inside manage() rolls back then re-panics.
decisions.rationale: The DB transaction must be cleaned up even on panic, but the panic must propagate so upper-layer recovery (Chi middleware, test harness) still sees it.

code_examples.scenario: Adapter method running inside a transaction, reusing an existing one from ctx
